evidence: grep confirms literal token '$ARG' at line 8, not '$ARGUMENTS'
description: Command uses literal $ARG instead of $ARGUMENTS placeholder so user-supplied bug description is never substituted
suggested_fix: Replace $ARG with $ARGUMENTS

evidence: File has no YAML frontmatter block at all (no name/description fields)
description: Command file has no frontmatter, so it cannot register name/description for /help or discovery
suggested_fix: Add a YAML frontmatter block with name and description

description: References an 'orchestrate-review' skill and Task subagent_type 'review', neither of which exists anywhere in the repo
suggested_fix: Create the missing agent/skill, or replace with an existing subagent_type

evidence: find confirms no docs/ directory exists anywhere under perf/; grep shows 14 in-scope perf artifacts (perf.md, 8 skills, 5 agents) cite it as canonical
description: perf.md and 13 other perf agents/skills cite docs/perf-requirements.md as the canonical contract / source of truth, but the file does not exist anywhere in the plugin
suggested_fix: Add docs/perf-requirements.md (and docs/perf-research-methodology.md) to the perf plugin, or remove the references

evidence: frontmatter name: perf-investigation-logger; parent directory: investigation-logger
description: Frontmatter name does not match parent directory name, violating the open Agent Skills spec MUST requirement
suggested_fix: Rename directory to perf-investigation-logger or set name: investigation-logger

evidence: find confirms artifacts-builder/ contains only SKILL.md and plugin.json, no scripts/ directory
description: Skill instructs running scripts/init-artifact.sh and scripts/bundle-artifact.sh, neither of which exists on disk
suggested_fix: Add the referenced scripts, or rewrite the skill body to not depend on them

evidence: tools: frontmatter lists Read, Write, Edit, Task, Bash(git/npm/pnpm/yarn/cargo/go/pytest/mvn/gradle/node:*) -- no Grep, no Bash(grep:*)
description: Body instructs identifying hotspots 'via repo-map or grep' but neither Grep nor any Bash(grep:*) scope is declared in tools
suggested_fix: Add Grep (or Bash(grep:*)) to the tools list

evidence: grep confirms unquoted <skill-name>/<pre|post|complete> substitution at line 28
description: python3 "$SB_CLI" simulate <skill-name> --cwd "$PWD" --timing <pre|post|complete> substitutes user-supplied values directly into the shell command line with no quoting
suggested_fix: Quote the substituted values and validate skill-name against known subscriptions before interpolation

evidence: grep confirms unquoted [skill] substitution at line 33
description: python3 "$SB_CLI" simulate [skill] --cwd "$PWD" substitutes a suggestion-derived [skill] value directly into the shell command with no quotes
suggested_fix: Quote the substituted value and validate against known subscriptions before interpolation

evidence: grep confirms literal '$ARGUMENTS' inside a single-quoted JS string at line 59; allowed-tools includes Bash(node:*)
description: const args = argumentParser.parseArguments('$ARGUMENTS'); splices the raw $ARGUMENTS placeholder into a JS string literal executed via node with no escaping
suggested_fix: Pass arguments via an environment variable or JSON-encoded argv array instead of raw string interpolation into source code

evidence: grep confirms unquoted ${ARGUMENTS} interpolation at line 59
description: RESUME_MODE=$([ "${ARGUMENTS}" != "${ARGUMENTS%--resume*}" ] && echo true || echo false) interpolates raw ${ARGUMENTS} into a bash test expression with no quoting/sanitization guidance
suggested_fix: Quote ${ARGUMENTS} defensively and validate its contents before use in a shell conditional

evidence: grep confirms literal '$ARGUMENTS' inside a single-quoted JS string at line 81; allowed-tools includes Bash(node:*)
description: const args = '$ARGUMENTS'.split(' '); splices the raw $ARGUMENTS placeholder into a JS string literal executed via node with no escaping
suggested_fix: Pass arguments via an environment variable or JSON-encoded argv array instead of raw string interpolation into source code

description: runBenchmark(command, options) passes a caller-supplied string straight to execSync (shell-invoking); legitimate benchmark-runner design (identical file duplicated at audit-project/lib/perf/benchmark-runner.js and ship/lib/perf/benchmark-runner.js)
suggested_fix: Consider execFileSync with an argv array where the benchmark command is a fixed executable, or document the trust boundary explicitly

description: runProfiling builds command via profiler.buildCommand() and runs it with execSync; same shell-invocation pattern as benchmark-runner.js (also duplicated at audit-project/lib and ship/lib)
suggested_fix: Consider execFileSync with an argv array

description: fileExists/fileContains condition types accept absolute or ~-expanded paths from skill-bus.json and read their contents for pattern matching; a locally-authored config could point this at sensitive files
suggested_fix: Restrict path resolution to the project directory, or document the trust assumption that skill-bus.json is locally authored

description: DEBUG_LOG_FILE = "/tmp/security-warnings-log.txt" is a hardcoded absolute path outside the repo/project directory that the hook appends debug messages to
suggested_fix: Write debug logs under the plugin's own state directory (e.g. ~/.claude/...) rather than a shared /tmp path

description: runBenchmark spreads the full process.env plus caller-supplied env into the child process environment before execSync, forwarding any secrets present in the parent environment
suggested_fix: Pass only an explicit allowlist of environment variables to the child process

description: detectBranchStrategy/detectMainBranch use exec/execAsync (shell-invoking) with fixed literal git commands, no interpolated input
suggested_fix: Use execFile with an argv array for consistency with the rest of the codebase

description: Reads non-secret configuration env vars (AI_STATE_DIR, PLUGIN_ROOT, PERF_EXPERIMENT, etc.) to steer state-directory/platform detection; same pattern repeats in several other perf/lib files
suggested_fix: No action needed; documented for visibility

description: Session state is written under ~/.claude/security_warnings_state_<id>.json rather than the project directory; standard convention for this plugin family, not attacker-influenced
suggested_fix: No action needed

suggested_fix: Extract the shared lib/ into a single internal package the three plugins depend on, or document the intentional vendoring
